programmes.show_description: Der Tag: Hawkeye and BJ place a toe tag on a passed-out Frank as part of a drunken practical joke, but the joke backfires on them when he is accidentally shipped out to the front lines.
Hawkeye: Hawkeye is rescued by a Korean family when he is injured in a jeep accident. He knows how dangerous it can be to fall asleep with a concussion, so he talks to the family non-stop.
Some 38th Parallels: Frank becomes increasingly paranoid that the locals are stealing the camp's garbage, so he decides to auction off 150 pounds of it.
The Novocaine Mutiny: Frank charges Hawkeye with mutiny, but they each have different views of the circumstances leading to the court-martial.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Acting out: Matthew decides he wants to become an actor, but Henry is concerned his son will make a fool of himself during a college production.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
As Difficult As ABC: Brian Baldwin, a college basketball star, is diagnosed with cardiac insufficiency. After losing his scholarship he's forced to deal with his illiteracy.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Child Of God: Jonathan and Mark try to reunite Marsha with her father but he refuses to see her for having a child out of wedlock. But Marsha doesn't have much time left on Earth.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

The Return Of The Masked Rider: Jonathan and Mark help ageing movie heroes confront the gang, Satan's Helpers. They must persuade a boxer not to throw a big fight when the gang bullies him.
Song Of The Wild West: Jonathan and Mark visit a country-western bar where they meet a singer hoping to follow in her mother's footsteps. A broken family also needs Jonathan's assistance.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Pride of Walnut Grove: Mary worries that she will disappoint Walnut Grove if she doesn't place first in the state maths competition after the community pays her way to represent them.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: A Matter of Faith: Caroline sends her family on ahead to the picnic grounds for the weekend while she nurses a nasty cut to her leg that becomes infected.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Runaway Caboose: At a train station, Mary, Laura and Carl wander into a caboose. Without warning, the caboose begins to move, and it is soon on a crash course with an oncoming train.
The Recluse: Jason befriends a mysterious recluse who loves music, and Ben leaves home to look for a new job.
The Warrior: The startling history of Walton's Mountain leads to the discovery of an ancient burial ground.
Catch of the Day: While sand and mud create earthy problems around the village, Niamh takes a radical decision to solve a problem of her own.
Something Old: Jean and Eddie's big day will certainly be one they will never forget! Meanwhile, Tom considers visiting his father in New Zealand, and Donald tries to help a young orphan.
Eleanor is furious when Lady Cora arranges a meeting between the Thrift and Sir Herbert Hamilton and the governors of the East London Hospital.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Genuine Article: Rumpole defends an unknown artist who is accused of forging a famous painting.
Mulcahy's War: Frank claims a patient shot himself to avoid combat and calls Father Mulcahy. Troubled by his lack of experience at the front, he accompanies Radar to an aid station there.
The Korean Surgeon: Instead of turning him in as a prisoner of war, Hawkeye and B.J. pull some strings to get a wounded North Korean surgeon a spot on the staff.
Hawkeye Get Your Gun: Hawkeye and Potter provide assistance at a Korean clinic, and run into an enemy attack on the way home. Klinger poses as a Gypsy to help get his discharge papers.
The Colonel's Horse: Col. Potter leaves for some R&R and asks Radar to look after his mare. Margaret asks Hawkeye to operate on her appendix.
Hawk's Nightmare: Havoc ensues when Radar removes a spirit post from the road. When a man is brought in for medical attention, he refuses surgery unless the camp spirits are exorcised.
Exorcism: Hawkeye is surprised to learn that he has been sleepwalking. All he knows is that he's been particularly depressed about the young age of the soldiers he is trying to keep alive.
Unforgettable Characters: Radar becomes an aspiring writer and begins writing his impressions of the camp. Hawkeye and BJ give Frank a surprise birthday present that only he will appreciate.
K Is For Kill Part 2: Tiger By The Tail: With many of the assailants accounted for, the team are faced with tracking down the surviving agents before they start World War III. Part 2 of 2.
Love All: Steed and Tara investigate an unlikely suspect who is suddenly attracting the romantic affections of bureaucrats who are too keen to share their top secret information with her.
Mulcahy's War: Frank claims a patient shot himself to avoid combat and calls Father Mulcahy. Troubled by his lack of experience at the front, he accompanies Radar to an aid station there.
The Korean Surgeon: Instead of turning him in as a prisoner of war, Hawkeye and B.J. pull some strings to get a wounded North Korean surgeon a spot on the staff.
Hawkeye Get Your Gun: Hawkeye and Potter provide assistance at a Korean clinic, and run into an enemy attack on the way home. Klinger poses as a Gypsy to help get his discharge papers.
Julie: Henry's teenage daughter Julie comes to stay for a couple of days, but tensions soon mount, and Henry and Matthew soon question whether three is a crowd.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Match Made In Heaven: Jonathan's friend Scotty, a quadriplegic, wonders if his relationship with a woman he likes can succeed.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Banker And Bum: Melvyn, a cold-hearted tycoon swaps places with Willie, a kind-hearted homeless man to see what it would be like if they each lived different lives.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Teleshopping.
One Fresh Batch Of Lemonade: Part 1: Jonathan steps in to help Deke, a resentful young athlete whose legs are amputated below the knee following a tragic motorcycle accident.
One Fresh Batch Of Lemonade: Part 2: Jonathan sets out to restore Deke's faith in himself with the help of a young gymnast.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Troublemaker: When Miss Beadle is deemed unable to control some of the older boys in her class, Mrs Oleson convinces the school board to replace her with a firmer, male hand.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Long Road Home: Charles and Mr Edwards take a dangerous job handling highly explosive liquid dynamite.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: For My Lady: Caroline and the girls think the worst when Charles begins to behave mysteriously and lies about the amount of time he has spent doing carpentry with an attractive neighbour.
The Seashore: The Waltons go on vacation to the Baldwins' beach cottage, where they meet a mysterious English girl. Ben tries to enjoy the carefree life when he is left home alone.
The Volunteer: John and Olivia must accept that Erin is growing up when G.W. Haines proposes marriage.
Moving Out: Emma has had enough of her father's strange behaviour towards Niamh, but what will Ballykissangel's gossips think of her moving in with Danny?
The Great Leap Forward: The residents of Ormston welcome a new face. Donald and Deborah set up a mobile blood bank to try and find a match for teenage haemophiliac Abel Marl.
Dr Robert Bramwell's attempts to match his daughter Eleanor with an old army friend seem doomed to failure. Could a boxer with a head injury bring Eleanor and Major Hyde together in the end?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Golden Thread: Rumpole finds himself embroiled in the world of politics when he flies to Africa to defend a former pupil who has been charged with murder.
38 Across: Flummoxed by a crossword puzzle, Hawkeye persuades his old friend, Tippy Brook - an expert at puzzles, to come to the camp.
Ping Pong: The 4077th hosts a traditional Korean wedding, much to Frank's objections. Colonel Potter runs into an old friend who has put his troops in jeopardy to receive a promotion.
End Run: A former football player is distraught by the resulting surgery after a severe leg injury. Frank goes behind Colonel Potter's back to set up a boxing match between Klinger and Zale.
Lionel is determined to stop Jacko's relationship with his daughter Leslie, but a chance meeting between Jacko and brother Reg may do the job for him.
Lionel's daughter Leslie is on a forced separation from Jacko in America. Meanwhile, after a spell in hospital, it appears that Jacko's old charm is not working with the ladies.
Jacko's in turmoil, having received both a declaration of love from Lesley and a perceived come-on from Veronica - her mother! Lionel's preoccupied with a loan for the business.
Complex: The Avengers travel to Canada on a mission to discover the identity of a member of the KGB. Meanwhile, Purdey finds herself in danger when she is trapped inside a building.
